question_text: Two different foods have the same mass but different energy contents. Explain why they might have different amounts of energy, and discuss which macronutrient composition would provide more energy.
correct_answer: Different foods contain different proportions of carbohydrates, proteins, and fats. Since fats provide 9 kcal/g while carbohydrates and proteins provide 4 kcal/g, a food with higher fat content will have more energy per gram. Therefore, two foods of equal mass will have different total energy if one contains more fat and less carbohydrate.
explanation: This requires students to understand that energy content depends on macronutrient composition, not just mass, and to explain why fats are more energy-dense than other macronutrients.
